task.title: Implement post-commit CodeWiki GC capability
task.summary: Add a source-owned GC application/tool path that classifies and purges eligible CodeWiki artifacts only after archive commit proof and restore-ledger emission.

goal.outcome: Agents and adapters can invoke a safe CodeWiki GC dry-run/purge capability instead of manually deleting tracked `.codewiki` artifacts.
acceptance: GC dry-run reports eligible tracked builds/validation/runtime artifacts without deleting them.
Tracked purge requires archive commit SHA/tree proof and refuses to purge without it.
Tracked purge writes a restore ledger with removed paths and `git restore --source=<archive-sha> -- <path>` commands before deletion.
GC does not purge fail/block/current-policy/current-publication validation reports or active unconsumed handoffs.
Ignored runtime/session handoffs can be cleaned under runtime policy with clear reporting.
Pi adapter exposes the GC capability through a typed tool schema.
Main CodeWiki skill names agent ownership of `.codewiki` GC and the archive commit -> GC commit protocol.
Implementation/task-close output rules require post-commit GC review or explicit defer/block evidence after close/publication commit proof.
Validation/publication guidance distinguishes GC ledger proof from validation/content proof and forbids pre-commit tracked purge.
Tool references include the GC capability and restore-ledger safety fields.
Prompt asset tests assert GC enforcement text appears in relevant skill assets.
non_goals: Do not change task-close validation policy except where needed to require GC safety inputs.
Do not push, publish, or remove remote history.
Do not make generated graph state the source of purge truth.
Do not implement GC deletion logic in skill text.
Do not create container/umbrella roadmap tasks.
Do not weaken fresh validation or task-close gates.
verification: Run stale-reference/alignment/changed audits for touched docs and source.
Run npm run test:smoke and npm run typecheck.

exit_criteria: GC tool supports dry-run/purge and archive proof gate.
Skill assets enforce post-commit GC review/defer evidence.
Implementation build exists before validation handoff.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Implementation validation requires fresh context and checked content proof.

next_loop.reason: The next gateway must independently validate implementation evidence and cite checked content proof.

requirements.text: Tracked `.codewiki` artifacts must not be purged until an archive commit containing revive context exists.
GC must write restore evidence that names archive commit/tree, removed paths, and restore commands before deleting tracked artifacts from the hot tree.
Agent skills must make post-commit GC review mandatory at close/publication boundaries and require block/defer evidence when skipped.
A public source-owned GC path must support dry-run, scoped purge, tracked artifact gates, and ignored runtime cleanup without manual file deletion.

audit_reports: CodeWiki audit: warning; task pass; stale-reference pass; warnings limited to graph health for active TASK-100 and generated-file changed state before publication.
summary: Implemented TASK-100 post-commit CodeWiki GC capability and skill enforcement

evidence: GC behavior is exercised with a temp repo fixture containing graph purgeable classes, tracked build/validation files, runtime handoff file, and a real Git archive commit/tree.
Prompt asset tests guard skill-enforced GC flow text so future skill edits cannot silently remove the invariant.
Package smoke test ensures Pi registered tool and skill/app tool catalogs stay aligned.
notes: All listed checks passed in current dirty implementation worktree.
Changed audit warns about generated graph/task views changed, expected because CodeWiki builds/tasks were refreshed and must be included or regenerated before publication.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Added src/application/gc.ts source-owned GC use case with dry-run/purge, archive proof verification, restore ledger creation, graph-purgeable tracked candidates, runtime handoff cleanup, and safe path normalization.
Added src/application/tools/gc.ts application tool executor with optional rebuild after mutation.
Registered codewiki_gc in Pi adapter schema/index and application/skill tool catalogs.
Updated CodeWiki skills to make post-commit GC review part of main, implementation, planning, validation, and tool-reference flows.
Updated system KB to document archive commit -> GC commit protocol, API capability, graph advisory status, and validation/report lifecycle boundaries.
notes: No manual tracked `.codewiki` artifact deletion was performed. New GC path was tested against temporary fixtures only.
Actual dogfood cleanup should happen after a commit containing this implementation and current CodeWiki artifacts exists; then run codewiki_gc dry-run/purge with that commit SHA/tree.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: GC behavior is exercised with a temp repo fixture containing graph purgeable classes, tracked build/validation files, runtime handoff file, and a real Git archive commit/tree.
Prompt asset tests guard skill-enforced GC flow text so future skill edits cannot silently remove the invariant.
Package smoke test ensures Pi registered tool and skill/app tool catalogs stay aligned.
code_change_evidence: Added src/application/gc.ts source-owned GC use case with dry-run/purge, archive proof verification, restore ledger creation, graph-purgeable tracked candidates, runtime handoff cleanup, and safe path normalization.
Added src/application/tools/gc.ts application tool executor with optional rebuild after mutation.
Registered codewiki_gc in Pi adapter schema/index and application/skill tool catalogs.
Updated CodeWiki skills to make post-commit GC review part of main, implementation, planning, validation, and tool-reference flows.
Updated system KB to document archive commit -> GC commit protocol, API capability, graph advisory status, and validation/report lifecycle boundaries.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: GC dry-run reports eligible tracked builds/validation/runtime artifacts without deleting them.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/hot-retention.test.mjs creates graph purgeable build+validation and runtime handoff fixtures, asserts dry-run reports 2 tracked + 1 runtime and keeps tracked files.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tracked purge requires archive commit SHA/tree proof and refuses to purge without it.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/hot-retention.test.mjs calls tracked purge without archive proof, asserts status blocked, missing archive_sha reason, and validation file remains.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tracked purge writes a restore ledger with removed paths and git restore commands before deletion.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/hot-retention.test.mjs commits archive fixture, passes archive_sha/tree_sha, asserts purge deletes tracked files and ledger records archive commit plus git restore command for build path.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: GC does not purge fail/block/current-policy/current-publication validation reports or active unconsumed handoffs.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: src/application/gc.ts only uses graph purgeable classes for tracked candidates and additionally requires validation JSON verdict pass; fail/block/current-policy/current-publication reports are not eligible if not graph purgeable/pass.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Ignored runtime/session handoffs can be cleaned under runtime policy with clear reporting.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: src/application/gc.ts scopes runtime cleanup to .codewiki/runtime/session-handoffs files and tests/smoke/hot-retention.test.mjs verifies runtime-only purge without archive proof.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Pi adapter exposes the GC capability through a typed tool schema.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: src/adapters/pi/schemas.ts defines codewikiGcToolInputSchema; src/adapters/pi/index.ts registers codewiki_gc and delegates to src/application/tools/gc.ts; package smoke verifies tool registration/catalog delegation.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Main CodeWiki skill names agent ownership of .codewiki GC and archive commit -> GC commit protocol.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: skills/codewiki/SKILL.md lists codewiki_gc, defines post-commit tracked GC invariant, and prompt asset smoke asserts GC wording.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Implementation/task-close output rules require post-commit GC review or explicit defer/block evidence after close/publication commit proof.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: skills/codewiki-implementation/SKILL.md and references/tools.md require codewiki_gc dry-run after close/publication commit and defer/block evidence when not safe.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Validation/publication guidance distinguishes GC ledger proof from validation/content proof and forbids pre-commit tracked purge.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: skills/codewiki-validation/SKILL.md and references/tools.md state GC ledger is restoration evidence only and pre-commit tracked GC blocks close/publication readiness.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tool references include the GC capability and restore-ledger safety fields.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: skills/codewiki/references/tool-catalog.md documents codewiki_gc, archive_sha/tree_sha inputs, post-commit protocol, and restore ledger commands.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Prompt asset tests assert GC enforcement text appears in relevant skill assets.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/prompt-assets.test.mjs asserts codewiki_gc, post-commit, archive_sha, dry-run, and GC restore ledger wording.

closure_brief.user_intent: Enforce agent-managed CodeWiki garbage collection after commits so Git remains the cold restore archive and hot .codewiki state does not pile up.
implemented_changes: Added source-owned post-commit GC use case/tool and Pi registration.
Added restore-ledger and archive SHA/tree proof gates for tracked purge.
Added runtime handoff cleanup path for ignored runtime artifacts.
Updated skill contracts and prompt tests so agents must run or defer GC review after close/publication commits.
Documented GC protocol and API ownership in system KB.
acceptance_evidence: All TASK-100 acceptance criteria mapped to tests or source/skill evidence.
npm run check:architecture, test:smoke, test:features, test:pack, and typecheck passed.
remaining_risks: Dogfood cleanup still awaits archive commit proof and fresh validation/task-close process.

risks: Generated graph currently reports expected warnings until TASK-100 receives implementation validation/closure evidence.
Actual repository GC should not run until archive commit/tree proof exists and must not amend away that commit.

restore.note: Restored history is reference material until promoted into active knowledge or roadmap truth.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: feat(codewiki): add post-commit garbage collection
commit.body: Add a source-owned codewiki_gc tool with dry-run, archive proof gating, restore-ledger emission, and runtime handoff cleanup. Enforce the post-commit GC invariant in CodeWiki skills and tests so tracked .codewiki artifacts are purged only after an archive commit exists.
trailers: CodeWiki-Checks: npm run typecheck, node tests/smoke/hot-retention.test.mjs && node tests/smoke/prompt-assets.test.mjs, node tests/smoke/package-smoke.test.mjs, npm run test:smoke, npm run check:architecture, npm run test:features, npm run test:pack, codewiki_audit profiles=[alignment,task,stale-reference,changed] task_id=TASK-100
commit.validation_ref_policy: replace <pending-validation> with validation report ref before commit

pr.title: feat(codewiki): add post-commit garbage collection
pr.body: Add a source-owned codewiki_gc tool with dry-run, archive proof gating, restore-ledger emission, and runtime handoff cleanup. Enforce the post-commit GC invariant in CodeWiki skills and tests so tracked .codewiki artifacts are purged only after an archive commit exists.
